title: Dividend Aristocrats: Yield, Payout & Growth Analysis 2026
description: 67 companies with 25+ year dividend streaks — but which ones are actually worth buying today? Yields, valuations, and growth compared.

schemas.headline: Dividend Aristocrats: Yield, Payout & Growth Analysis 2026
schemas.description: Complete analysis of all 67 S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats — companies with 25+ consecutive years of dividend increases. Current yields, P/E ratios, market caps, and sector breakdown.

acceptedAnswer.text: A Dividend Aristocrat is a company in the S&P 500 index that has increased its dividend payout every year for at least 25 consecutive years. As of March 2026, there are 67 Dividend Aristocrats. These companies have demonstrated consistent profitability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders through decades of market cycles, recessions, and economic uncertainty.

mainEntity.name: Which Dividend Aristocrat has the highest yield?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of March 2026, Altria Group (MO) has the highest dividend yield among Dividend Aristocrats at 6.37%, followed by T. Rowe Price (TROW) at 5.75% and Franklin Resources (BEN) at 5.03%. However, high yield alone isn't always the best indicator — investors should also consider payout ratio sustainability, earnings growth, and the company's competitive position.

Dividend Aristocrats have historically outperformed the broader S&P 500 with lower volatility. Their 25+ year track record of dividend increases demonstrates financial discipline and durable competitive advantages. They provide a growing income stream that can help offset inflation. However, past performance doesn't guarantee future results, and some aristocrats trade at premium valuations. The ProShares S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats ETF (NOBL) tracks this group.

mainEntity.name: How many Dividend Aristocrats are there?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of March 2026, there are 67 Dividend Aristocrats in the S&P 500. The list is reviewed annually by S&P Dow Jones Indices. Companies can be added when they reach 25 consecutive years of dividend increases, and removed if they cut or freeze their dividend, or if they exit the S&P 500 index. Industrials (16 companies) and Consumer Staples (15 companies) are the most represented sectors.

mainEntity.name: What is the average yield of Dividend Aristocrats?

acceptedAnswer.text: The average dividend yield across all 67 Dividend Aristocrats is 2.42% as of March 2026. This compares to roughly 1.3% for the S&P 500 as a whole. Yields range from 0.36% (West Pharmaceutical Services) to 6.37% (Altria Group). The highest-yielding sectors among aristocrats are Utilities and Consumer Staples, while Information Technology aristocrats tend to have lower yields.
